name: Metiamide
description: Metiamide is a histamine H2-receptor antagonist developed from another H2 antagonist, burimamide. It was an intermediate compound in the development of the successful anti-ulcer drug cimetidine.
indication: Potential in the treatment and the management of acid-reflux disorders (GERD), peptic ulcer disease, heartburn, and acid indigestion.
mechanismOfAction: Metiamide binds to an H<sub>2</sub>-receptor located on the basolateral membrane of the gastric parietal cell, blocking histamine effects. This competitive inhibition results in reduced gastric acid secretion and a reduction in gastric volume and acidity.
pharmacodynamics: Metiamide is a histamine H<sub>2</sub>-receptor antagonist. It reduces basal and nocturnal gastric acid secretion and a reduction in gastric volume, acidity, and amount of gastric acid released in response to stimuli including food, caffeine, insulin, betazole, or pentagastrin. Metiamide inhibits many of the isoenzymes of the hepatic CYP450 enzyme system. Other actions of Metiamide include an increase in gastric bacterial flora such as nitrate-reducing organisms.

interactions.name: Dasatinib
interactions.description: Metiamide can cause a decrease in the absorption of Dasatinib resulting in a reduced serum concentration and potentially a decrease in efficacy.

interactions.name: Delavirdine
interactions.description: Metiamide can cause a decrease in the absorption of Delavirdine resulting in a reduced serum concentration and potentially a decrease in efficacy.

interactions.name: Pazopanib
interactions.description: Metiamide can cause a decrease in the absorption of Pazopanib resulting in a reduced serum concentration and potentially a decrease in efficacy.

interactions.name: Risedronate
interactions.description: Metiamide can cause an increase in the absorption of Risedronate resulting in an increased serum concentration and potentially a worsening of adverse effects.

interactions.name: Ketoconazole
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Ketoconazole can be decreased when it is combined with Metiamide.

interactions.name: Mesalazine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Mesalazine can be decreased when used in combination with Metiamide.

interactions.name: Benzylpenicilloyl Polylysine
interactions.description: Metiamide may decrease effectiveness of Benzylpenicilloyl Polylysine as a diagnostic agent.

interactions.name: Hyaluronidase
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Hyaluronidase can be decreased when used in combination with Metiamide.
